1.        What is the difference between SOQL and SOSL.
SOQL → query records from specific object with relationships
SOSL → search text across multiple objects and fields
        2.        What are the Batch apex methods.
start(), execute(), finish()
        3.        Can we call future from other future.
No, not allowed
        4.        What the return type of future.
void only
        5.        What are the decorators in lwc.
@api, @track, @wire
        6.        What is LMS.
Lightning Message Service used for communication between unrelated components
        7.        How to communicate parent to child and child to parent and unrelated components in lwc.
Parent → child → @api
Child → parent → CustomEvent
Unrelated → LMS
        8.        What is the difference @Wire and imperative methods.
@wire → reactive, auto call, only for read
Imperative → manual call, used for read/write
        9.        Can we use button with wire.
No, use imperative
        10.        What is the meaning cacheable= true.
Enables client side caching, improves performance, only for read
        11.        How many types of triggers and what are there.
Before → insert, update, delete
After → insert, update, delete, undelete
        12.        What is the syntax of LMS.
import { publish, subscribe, MessageContext } from ‘lightning/messageService’;
import CHANNEL from ‘@salesforce/messageChannel/ChannelName__c’;
        13.        How to pass the values from child to parent and what is the syntax.
this.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent(‘eventname’,{ detail: value }));
        14.        Explain about REST API.
Used to integrate external systems using HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
        15.        What is Remote site settings and named credentials.
Remote Site → whitelist external URLs
Named Credential → store endpoint + auth, no need to hardcode
        16.        Explain difference between queueable apex and Future methods.
Queueable → supports chaining, complex objects, better monitoring
Future → simple, no chaining, primitive params only
        17.        Can we use dml operation with wire.
No, wire is read only
        18.        What is LDS.
Lightning Data Service → fetch and cache Salesforce data without Apex
        19.        Write the query for display the accounts name whose contacts more than 5.
SELECT Name FROM Account WHERE Id IN (SELECT AccountId FROM Contact GROUP BY AccountId HAVING COUNT(Id) > 5)
        20.        Write trigger for prevent the duplication of accounts with same email.
trigger AccountDuplicate on Account (before insert, before update) {
Set emails = new Set();
for(Account acc : Trigger.new){
if(acc.Email__c != null){
emails.add(acc.Email__c);
}
}
Map<String, Account> existing = new Map<String, Account>();
for(Account a : [SELECT Id, Email_c FROM Account WHERE Email_c IN :emails]){
existing.put(a.Email__c, a);
}
for(Account acc : Trigger.new){
if(existing.containsKey(acc.Email__c)){
acc.Email__c.addError(‘Duplicate Email not allowed’);
}
}
}
        21.        what is trigger.new.
List of new records in insert/update
        22.        What is trigger.old
List of old records in update/delete
        23.        Explain Difference before trigger and after trigger.
Before → used to update/validate before save
After → used when record is already saved, for related operations
        24.        Why we use trigger.oldmap and trigger.new map.
Used for fast access using Id and compare old vs new values
        25.        Can we call batch apex from trigger.
Yes, but not recommended (use async carefully)
        26.        Can we call out directly in trigger and if not why.
No, triggers are synchronous and callouts not allowed → use future/queueable
        27.        Explain collections and why we use
List → ordered collection
Set → unique values
Map → key-value pair
Used for bulk processing and performance
        28.        How to avoid the recursion in triggers.
Use static boolean flag or static set of processed record Ids
        29.        Explain trigger best practices
One trigger per object
Bulkify code
Avoid SOQL/DML inside loops
Use handler class
Use collections

Handle recursion
        30.        What is the batch size in batch apex class.
Default 200, can be set up to 2000
        31.        If one chunk is fail in batch apex entire batch is fail
No, only that chunk fails, rest executes (partial success)

 Named Credentials: They provide a centralized way to store and manage authentication credentials for external services. When you use Named Credentials, Salesforce handles the authentication for Apex callouts, which means your code doesn’t have to include any authentication logic. Additionally, Named Credentials allow you to skip Remote Site Settings for the sites defined in them1. They also support maintaining endpoint URLs and authentication parameters separately, making maintenance easier1.
Remote Site Settings: These are used to authorize external endpoints for callouts from Salesforce. Before making any callout to an external URL, that URL must be declared in the Remote Site Settings. Unlike Named Credentials, Remote Site Settings do not store any authentication details; they simply enable access to the specified external sites2.

Assignment Modules:
Module 1: Apex Trigger (Account Management)

Develop an Apex trigger named AccountManagementTrigger that performs the following actions when an Account record is inserted or updated:

Automatic Contact Creation: If an Account record's Industry field is 'Technology', automatically create a new Contact record associated with the Account.

Ownership Assignment: Assign ownership of the Account record based on the Industry field:

'Technology': Assign to a specific user (configurable in a Custom Setting).

Other Industries: Assign to a default owner (e.g., a designated user or a queue).

Module 2: Asynchronous Processing (Future Method)

Implement a Future method (@future) named ProcessAccountAsync to perform asynchronous processing for Account records. The method should:

Update a custom field on the Account record after a specified delay (configurable).

Module 3: Batch Apex (Data Processing)

Develop a Batch Apex class named AccountBatchProcessor to handle bulk processing of Account records. The class should:

Query and process Accounts based on custom criteria (e.g., Accounts with specific attributes or conditions, Deactivate the account if there is no activity in last 30 days OR Send and email if customer birthday today).

Implement error handling and logging within the batch execution.

Module 4: Integration (Callouts)

Integrate Salesforce with an external API using Apex callouts. Develop functionality to:

Make an HTTP callout to an external system based on Account data.

Process the response and update related records (e.g., Account or Contact).

Module 5: Test Classes (Code Coverage) – Good to have (if time permits)

Create comprehensive test classes (AccountManagementTriggerTest, AccountBatchProcessorTest, etc.) to achieve high code coverage for the implemented Apex classes. Write unit tests to cover positive and negative scenarios, bulk data operations, and error conditions.

Module 6: Error Handling and Logging(How this can be cascaded) 

Implement robust error handling and logging mechanisms within Apex classes. Use try-catch blocks and System.debug() statements to capture and handle exceptions effectively.

Module 7: Dynamic Configuration (Custom Settings) –  Good to have

Enhance the solution with dynamic configurations using Custom Settings or Custom Metadata Types. Implement configurable settings for user assignments, delay durations, API endpoints, etc.

Module 8: Performance Optimization – Good to have

Optimize Apex code for performance and scalability. Apply best practices such as selective querying, efficient data processing, and asynchronous processing patterns to improve overall system performance.

If all Tasks related to the Project have a Status of "Completed," the Project's Status should be set to "Completed."

If there are no Tasks related to the Project, the Project's Status should be set to "No Tasks."

 1) Record is read-only
        - This is because you are in an after insert/update trigger and the records are read only in that context as they have been written, but not committed, to the database.

2) Use only API key in named credentials
        - First, go to the New Named Credential screen. Specify the base endpoint ("http://api.jotform.com/"), set the Identity Type to "Named Principle," choose Authentication Protocol "Password Authentication," specify any random user name ("anonymous" should work), and type in the API key as your password. Uncheck "Generate Authorization Header" and check "Allow Merge Fields in HTTP Header", then save this Named Credential.

        Now, in your code, you can specify the API key using a merge field:

        HttpRequest req = new HttpRequest();
        req.setMethod('GET');
        req.setEndpoint('callout:jotform/user');
        req.setHeader('APIKEY', '{!$Credential.Password}');
        HttpResponse res = new Http().send(req);

        - !System.isFuture() && !System.isBatch()

4) We have a batch scheduled for every 2 minutes. While deploying same batch we are getting 'This Apex class has batch or future jobs pending or in progress' How to deploy same component without disturbing the batch schedule ?
        - To prevent the error from occurring navigate to Deployment Settings and enable 'Allow deployments of components when corresponding Apex jobs are pending or in progress'
5) Getting Error UNABLE_TO_LOCK_ROW 
        - When you have multiple jobs running that update the same set of records, it's recommended to lock the records using the FOR UPDATE keyword. It will ensure that the record is locked and no other job can update the same record at that point in time. The conflicting job will then wait untill the locking on the record is released and then perform the required operaiton. This will avoid conflicts between jobs that are running concurrently and will not throw up this exception. You can refer the below link to learn more about record locking.

 1. What is CTI Integration vs IVR?

* CTI (Computer Telephony Integration): Connects phone system with Salesforce for call controls like screen pop, call logging, etc.
* IVR (Interactive Voice Response): Voice menu system used before connecting to agent (press 1 for sales, etc.).
* Difference: IVR routes the call, CTI handles call interaction inside Salesforce.

6. Apex Method: Map of AccountId → List of Contact Ids:

apex
public static Map<Id, List<Id>> getAccountWithContactIds() {
    Map<Id, List<Id>> accountContactMap = new Map<Id, List<Id>>();
    for (Contact con : [SELECT Id, AccountId FROM Contact WHERE AccountId != null]) {
        if (!accountContactMap.containsKey(con.AccountId)) {
            accountContactMap.put(con.AccountId, new List<Id>());
        }
        accountContactMap.get(con.AccountId).add(con.Id);
    }
    return accountContactMap;
}

8. What is Event Propagation?

* Flow: Capturing → Target → Bubbling.
* Events bubble up DOM if bubbles: true.
* Events cross shadow DOM if composed: true.

9. Two Child Components (No Relation) → Talk via Grandparent:

* Child 1 dispatches event with bubbles: true, composed: true.
* Parent listens and re-dispatches new event.
* Grandparent listens to the re-dispatched event.
* No direct reference between children needed.

10. Default Value of Bubbles & Composed:

* bubbles: false
* composed: false

11. Meaning of Bubbles & Composed:

* bubbles: allows event to move up DOM tree.
* composed: allows event to escape Shadow DOM.
